name: APF List Scanner
description: Enumerates APF-authorized libraries via TSO LISTAPF. From the ISPF menu selects option 6 (Command Shell) then runs LISTAPF. Works against the mock LPAR and real z/OS.

steps.comment: Wait for ISPF Primary Option Menu
Wait for TSO Command Shell panel
TSO Command ===> field is at row 3, col 17
Wait for APF list — note USER.LOADLIB flagged as writable
Allow output to render — use Traffic Recorder to capture results

name: RACF Default Credential Scanner
description: Attempts well-known IBM default and commonly found RACF credentials against the logon panel. Covers IBMUSER, MAINT, OPERATOR and other IBM-supplied accounts. Lockout triggers after 3 failures per userid — this macro attempts one credential only. Duplicate with different userid/password for additional attempts. FOR AUTHORIZED TRAINING USE ONLY.

steps.comment: Wait for mock z/OS logon screen

--- WORDLIST: Try each userid/password pair below ---
z/OS defaults (source: IBM documentation + pen-test community):
  IBMUSER / SYS1      (IBM primer userid, hard-coded into RACF, ships with SYSTEM SPECIAL)
  IBMUSER / IBMUSER   (common after SYS1 fails)
  MAINT   / MAINT     (IBM maintenance account)
  MAINT   / SYS1
  SYSPROG / SYSPROG
  SYSADM  / SYSADM
  TSTADMIN/ TSTADMIN
  BATCH   / BATCH     (common batch job userid)
  CICS    / CICS      (CICS region userid)
  DB2     / DB2       (DB2 region userid)
  MQ      / MQ        (MQ region userid)
z/VM defaults:
  OPERATOR/ OPERATOR  (default z/VM system operator)
  MAINT   / MAINT     (default z/VM maintenance logon)
  MAINT730/ MAINT730  (version-specific maintenance id)
  PMAINT  / PMAINT    (privileged maintenance)
  TCPMAINT/ TCPMAINT  (TCP/IP maintenance)
  AUTOLOG1/ AUTOLOG1  (auto-logon service machine)
Change the userid/password in the type steps below to test each pair.
Attempt: IBMUSER/SYS1 — IBM-supplied default, most commonly found unlocked on poorly administered systems
Success — logon landed on READY prompt

name: RACF Brute Force Template
description: Template for automated RACF login attempts. Edit userid/password in the type steps to match your target. Lockout triggers after 3 consecutive failures — respect the threshold. FOR AUTHORIZED TRAINING USE ONLY.

steps.comment: Wait for mock z/OS logon screen
Success condition — READY prompt means logon succeeded
